aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Nearest-resize the calibration lens_shading_gain map and multiply it into gain-corrected RAW.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Meaningful only with a sensor/lens calibration map.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Lets downstream gating distinguish center and vignetted regions when a calibrated gain map exists.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: A uniform value of 1 is missing/neutral calibration, not proof of uniform optics.
aux_map_catalog.name: lens_gain
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose spatial lens-shading compensation and its noise amplification context.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Positive gain; 1 means neutral and values above 1 amplify signal and modeled noise.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Initialize ones and set calibrated defect coordinates to zero after local box-filter replacement.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Requires an accurate defect-pixel table.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Allows DNN and health logic to reduce trust in interpolated sensor samples.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: An all-one map means no table entries were supplied, not that the sensor has no defects.
aux_map_catalog.name: defect_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Mark pixels replaced from the defect table.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; 0 marks a corrected defect and 1 an unlisted pixel.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Argmax of SNR × non-saturation × low-signal × inter-plane-consistency weights; use the darkest fallback when every weight is zero.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Useful for multi-exposure inputs; single exposure is uniformly index 0.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Makes exposure switching auditable and can condition highlight/shadow processing.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: It is the dominant contributor, not a hard statement that other planes were unused.
aux_map_catalog.name: hdr_exposure_source
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Identify the exposure with the largest fusion weight at each pixel.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Categorical exposure-plane index 0..E-1.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Threshold every normalized exposure and reduce with logical any across the exposure axis.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applies to single and multi-exposure RAW.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Supports highlight-risk gating and prevents saturated structure from receiving high edge confidence.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: It does not mean the fused HDR result is clipped; a shorter plane may still recover the pixel.
aux_map_catalog.name: saturation
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Locate pixels saturated in any input exposure.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Binary [0,1]; 1 means at least one source plane crossed hdr_saturation_threshold.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Normalize threshold minus the maximum normalized exposure by the threshold and clip to [0,1].
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applies to the configured pseudo-RAW scale.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Provides a continuous highlight-risk signal beyond the binary saturation map.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: It is based on the brightest input plane, not fused-radiance headroom.
aux_map_catalog.name: clipping_distance
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Express remaining headroom below the saturation threshold.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; 0 is at/above threshold and larger values have more headroom.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Sum nonnegative fusion weights and divide by exposure count, then clip to [0,1].
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applies to single and multi-exposure inputs.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Allows output and DNN validity to fall where exposures are saturated, noisy, or inconsistent.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: This is a heuristic confidence and is not calibrated probability or HDR quality ground truth.
aux_map_catalog.name: hdr_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Summarize usable HDR fusion support.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger aggregate usable exposure support.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Compute per-pixel radiance standard deviation divided by mean absolute radiance, clipped to [0,1].
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Informative only for two or more exposure planes.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Can suppress unreliable HDR edges and reveal motion/flicker merge risk.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: No registration or optical flow is used; motion, JPEG differences, AE errors, and noise are conflated.
aux_map_catalog.name: ghost_motion_artifact
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose inter-plane radiance disagreement that can create ghosting.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means greater normalized disagreement.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: For Bayer use the measured/interpolated green plane; clear, IR, and mono layouts use their decoder-specific intensity.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applicable to all supported CFA modes.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Supports low-latency edge and temporal processing without waiting for display rendering.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Bayer green is a luma proxy, not colorimetric luminance.
aux_map_catalog.name: luma
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Provide a dense structural intensity channel close to the sensor domain.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Nonnegative linear intensity on the fused pseudo-RAW scale.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Interpolate C samples for RCCB/RCCC/RCCG; use fused signal for mono; emit zeros for Bayer/RGB-IR.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Only physically meaningful for native clear/mono CFA capture.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: May improve low-light structure when genuine clear pixels and calibration are available.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: CameraE2E Bayer and RGB proxy bridges do not create native clear measurements.
aux_map_catalog.name: clear_channel
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose clear-pixel signal for clear-filter CFA layouts.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Nonnegative linear clear signal; zero when the active CFA has no clear samples.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Interpolate IR samples in RGB-IR mode; emit zeros for Bayer, clear, and mono modes.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Only physically meaningful for native RGB-IR capture and crosstalk calibration.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Can let a model reason about IR illumination and visible-color contamination.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: An RGB-derived CameraE2E proxy is not a native IR measurement.
aux_map_catalog.name: ir_channel
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose interpolated infrared-pixel signal.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Nonnegative linear IR signal; zero when the CFA has no IR samples.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Combine CFA neighborhood support, horizontal/vertical gradient separation and local contrast, then mix with edge confidence and non-edge support.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Decoder-specific; valid as a relative diagnostic for supported CFA modes.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Supports artifact suppression and downweights uncertain color edges.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Not calibrated against demosaic ground truth and partly depends on later edge confidence.
aux_map_catalog.name: demosaic_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Estimate reliability of reconstructed color samples.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher indicates stronger reconstruction support.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Multiply normalized SNR, non-saturation, global WB-balance confidence, and HDR confidence.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applicable to decoded RGB but depends on calibration quality.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Can reduce reliance on chroma under low SNR, saturation, poor HDR support, or strong global imbalance.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Global channel imbalance is only a WB heuristic and can penalize legitimately colored scenes.
aux_map_catalog.name: color_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Estimate where color information is trustworthy.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger modeled color reliability.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Compute safe log(R/G) after the camera color matrix.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applicable when decoded camera RGB is meaningful.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Offers illumination/color cues with reduced dependence on overall brightness.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Sensitive to color matrix, demosaic error, clipping, and very small green values.
aux_map_catalog.name: log_r_over_g
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Represent red chromaticity relative to green.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Signed log ratio; 0 means R equals G.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Compute safe log(B/G) after the camera color matrix.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applicable when decoded camera RGB is meaningful.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Offers illumination/color cues with reduced dependence on overall brightness.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Sensitive to color matrix, demosaic error, clipping, and very small green values.
aux_map_catalog.name: log_b_over_g
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Represent blue chromaticity relative to green.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Signed log ratio; 0 means B equals G.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Compute IR divided by mean visible RGB plus IR.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Only meaningful for genuine RGB-IR input.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Can gate visible-color features where IR leakage is strong.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Uniform zero on Bayer is not evidence of an IR-free physical sensor.
aux_map_catalog.name: ir_contamination
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Estimate the fraction of signal attributable to IR.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means larger modeled IR contribution.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Sum shot, read, temperature/exposure dark-current, quantization, and calibration-residual variance; scale by lens_gain squared.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Requires representative sensor-noise and calibration coefficients.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Allows denoising, fast-path features, and DNN inputs to distinguish signal from noisy regions.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: CameraE2E bridge defaults are not a calibrated nuScenes camera noise model.
aux_map_catalog.name: noise_variance
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose modeled per-pixel uncertainty in the fused RAW signal.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Positive modeled variance in normalized signal units squared.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Compute signal/sqrt(variance), then map SNR to SNR/(SNR+1).
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Relative validity follows the supplied noise model.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Selected as the compact reliability channel in the stable RGB+Aux tensor and as a gate for edge/color confidence.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: It is not measured SNR and is optimistic or pessimistic when calibration coefficients are wrong.
aux_map_catalog.name: snr_map
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Provide bounded signal reliability.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger modeled signal relative to noise.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Divide luma gradient magnitude by sqrt(2 × modeled variance).
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Useful when both luma and the noise model are meaningful.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Helps distinguish structured transitions from noise-only gradients.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Large texture and demosaic artifacts can also produce high values.
aux_map_catalog.name: noise_normalized_gradient
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Measure local intensity change relative to expected noise.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Nonnegative, unbounded standardized gradient-like score.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Build a noise-weighted luma/R-G/B-G structure tensor, take sqrt of the largest eigenvalue, and normalize by the frame maximum.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applicable to all decoded inputs.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Supports boundary-aware image formation, sparse EdgePackets, and DNN spatial features.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Frame-max normalization prevents absolute comparison across unrelated runs without stored statistics.
aux_map_catalog.name: edge_strength
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Represent local structural gradient magnitude.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1] frame-relative strength.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Use half-angle atan2 of the structure-tensor off-diagonal and diagonal difference.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Meaningful where edge strength/confidence is sufficient.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Can support orientation-aware filtering or geometric feature extraction.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Orientation is unstable in flat, noisy, or isotropic texture regions.
aux_map_catalog.name: edge_orientation
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose the dominant local edge direction.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Cyclic radians in approximately [-pi/2, pi/2].

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Multiply structure-tensor anisotropy, MTF, exp(-0.5×PSF sigma squared), non-saturation, and SNR.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Depends on valid noise and optics calibration.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Expected to suppress noisy, saturated, blurred, or isotropic edges while retaining supported boundaries.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: A high value is not object-boundary probability or detector performance evidence.
aux_map_catalog.name: edge_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Estimate whether a detected edge is structurally and sensor-wise reliable.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger modeled edge reliability.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Robust-normalize strength and confidence with p1/p99 ranges and take their geometric mean.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applicable to decoded inputs with meaningful edge maps.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Selected for DNN/proposal experiments where strength alone admits noise and confidence alone can be weak at strong boundaries.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Percentile normalization makes values run-relative rather than calibrated probabilities.
aux_map_catalog.name: edge_evidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Combine edge magnitude and reliability into one compact feature.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1] frame-relative combined evidence.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Normalize luma and maximum chroma gradient by frame maxima and classify at threshold 0.12 as none/luma/chroma/both.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Applicable when decoded chroma differences are meaningful.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Can distinguish intensity boundaries from color-only transitions and demosaic-sensitive locations.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Fixed frame-relative thresholds are diagnostic and not universally calibrated.
aux_map_catalog.name: edge_type
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Separate luminance and chromatic edge evidence.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Categorical: 0 none, 1 luma, 2 chroma, 3 both.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Average edge confidence with edge strength normalized by the configured high-percentile strength threshold.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Relative within a frame and most meaningful around edges.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Can flag regions where detail-sensitive downstream features should be downweighted.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Texture scarcity, motion blur, defocus, and low contrast are not separated.
aux_map_catalog.name: blur_focus_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Summarize local focus support from strong reliable edges.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger local focus-like support.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Nearest-resize calibration.mtf_confidence_map, defaulting to one.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Meaningful only with measured/calibrated MTF data.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Allows known lens/sensor resolution loss to reduce edge confidence.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Uniform one is a neutral default, not measured perfect MTF.
aux_map_catalog.name: mtf_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Carry calibrated spatial MTF reliability into edge processing.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Normally [0,1]; one means no attenuation in the model.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Nearest-resize calibration.psf_sigma_map and clamp to nonnegative values.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Meaningful only with calibrated PSF data.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Lets downstream logic reason about spatially varying optical blur.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Zero is the default when no PSF map is supplied and is not a measured diffraction-free result.
aux_map_catalog.name: psf_sigma
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose calibrated local PSF blur scale.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Nonnegative sigma in calibration-defined sensor-pixel units.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Compute exp(-0.5 × psf_sigma squared).
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Depends entirely on PSF calibration.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Expected to reduce trust in edges where calibrated blur is large.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: A uniform one usually reflects missing/default PSF calibration.
aux_map_catalog.name: psf_blur_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Convert PSF width into a bounded sharpness prior.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; 1 at sigma 0 and decreasing with blur scale.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Subtract ghost_motion_artifact from edge_confidence and clip to [0,1].
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Useful as a diagnostic fusion of edge and HDR consistency.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Selected as a compact edge/optics cue for DNN experiments under blur and multi-exposure stress.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: It is not a fitted PSF likelihood despite the historical name.
aux_map_catalog.name: psf_edge_likelihood
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Prefer reliable edges that are not explained by HDR disagreement.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means confident edge with low inter-plane disagreement.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Generate a linear 0..1 vector over rows and reverse it for bottom-to-top readout.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Requires correct readout direction; row timestamps are available in Trace.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Allows models or diagnostics to condition on rolling-readout timing.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: No rolling-shutter geometric compensation is performed.
aux_map_catalog.name: rolling_row_fraction
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose normalized sensor-row readout position.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: One-dimensional [0,1] row coordinate.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Absolute difference between current linear perception luma and PreviousFrameState luma; zero without compatible state.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Requires correctly chained same-camera PreviousFrameState.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Supports low-latency motion/change awareness and frozen-frame diagnostics.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: No motion compensation; ego/object motion, AE, JPEG artifacts, and flicker are mixed.
aux_map_catalog.name: temporal_difference
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Expose change from the previous processed frame.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: Nonnegative linear intensity difference.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Compute exp(-temporal_difference / temporal_flicker_threshold).
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Requires compatible previous-frame state.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Allows downstream paths to downweight temporally unstable regions.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: It measures photometric consistency, not correspondence or optical flow confidence.
aux_map_catalog.name: temporal_consistency
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Convert frame change into bounded stability.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; 1 is unchanged and lower values indicate larger change.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Combine (1-temporal_consistency) × bright-light gate with HDR disagreement × saturation, then clip.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Most informative for bright light sources in a chained sequence or multi-exposure input.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Intended to expose LED/flicker risk to runtime control and downstream gating.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Not an LED classifier; motion, exposure change, and compression can raise it.
aux_map_catalog.name: led_flicker_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Highlight bright temporally unstable or HDR-disagreeing regions.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means stronger flicker-like evidence.

aux_map_catalog.algorithm: Map luma above 0.65 into a 0..1 bright gate and multiply by 1-0.5×flicker.
aux_map_catalog.applicability: Diagnostic for the configured linear intensity scale.
aux_map_catalog.expected_effect: Can help distinguish stable lights from unstable highlights.
aux_map_catalog.limitations: Brightness alone cannot distinguish lamps, reflections, sky, or saturated surfaces.
aux_map_catalog.name: light_source_confidence
aux_map_catalog.purpose: Represent bright light-source-like regions with a flicker penalty.
aux_map_catalog.value_semantics: [0,1]; higher means bright and relatively stable.

checks.evidence: three capture calls; every attached RAW has CameraE2E analog-voltage pseudo-RAW provenance
all capture inputs explicitly reject native sensor/ADC provenance

interframe_summary.interpretation: Adjacent processed nuScenes JPEGs were forward re-captured and grouped into unregistered temporal exposure cycles. This is motion/flicker/merge-risk evidence, not native RAW or same-instant HDR quality evidence.
interframe_summary.title: PerceptionISP nuScenes Inter-Frame HDR Stress
warnings: The exposure planes are different timestamps, not a same-instant sensor HDR bracket.
nuScenes source exposure, gain, CFA, black/white level, and radiometric response are unknown.
CameraE2E forward-simulates pseudo-RAW from processed JPEGs and cannot restore clipped source radiance.
No optical flow, ego-pose warp, geometric registration, or deghosting is applied.
Frame-specific seeds prevent repeated RNG streams, but CameraE2E does not preserve one fixed FPN realization across these independent calls.
Ghost-risk metrics are diagnostic; dynamic ghost risk is not required to exceed the static comparator for every scene.

interpretation: The displayed PNG previews and textual provenance in this GitHub preview were regenerated from the selected nuScenes JPEGs through CameraE2E sensor.volts pseudo-RAW and PerceptionISP. Lossless numerical arrays from the full local acceptance bundle are intentionally omitted; any retained array hashes or statistics document generation lineage but are not downloadable numerical evidence in this preview.

checks.evidence: nuScenes frames must not be relabeled as an HDR bracket
every frame must retain processed-JPEG pseudo-RAW provenance
source sensor metadata must remain unknown

temporal_summary.interpretation: This is a temporal/state and metadata-propagation stress test over nuScenes camera JPEGs. It is neither native RAW evidence nor a multi-exposure HDR evaluation.

camerae2e_ae_sequence_provenance.frame_shape_policy: one finite HxW voltage plane per frame
camerae2e_ae_sequence_provenance.temporal_noise_limitations: Shot, read, and fixed-pattern-noise RNG streams are not independently calibrated; temporal-noise or FPN claims are not supported.

raw_provenance.camerae2e_noise_limitation: shot/read/FPN RNG streams are not independently controlled or temporally calibrated

temporal_summary.title: PerceptionISP nuScenes Temporal Pseudo-RAW Example
warnings: nuScenes distributes processed, auto-exposed JPEG camera frames; source exposure, gain, CFA, and black/white levels are unavailable.
RGB-to-RAW conversion creates synthetic pseudo-RAW and cannot reconstruct clipped or quantized-away sensor information.
Ego poses and camera calibration are preserved as metadata, but this pipeline does not warp or register adjacent frames.
Temporal difference can therefore include ego motion, object motion, JPEG artifacts, and auto-exposure changes.
CameraE2E uses base-seed-plus-frame-index, but shot/read/FPN streams and temporal FPN persistence are not independently calibrated.

title: PerceptionISP Pipeline Showcase — nuScenes RGB → CameraE2E RAW
warnings: nuScenes inputs are processed auto-exposed JPEGs; source exposure, gain, WB, CFA and black/white levels remain unknown.
CameraE2E is a forward recapture simulator, not an inverse ISP and cannot restore clipped source radiance.
Adjacent-frame stacks are unregistered temporal HDR stress approximations, not simultaneous HDR brackets.
Only the same-frame AE bracket is a simulated multi-exposure comparator; neither path is native sensor RAW.
Aux confidence maps are model/heuristic diagnostics unless their required physical calibration is supplied.
